heading: Drug shortage list
label: 356e Drug shortage list
notes: Amendments2020—Subsec. (d). added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 116–1362016—Subsec. (b)(3)(E). , which directed substitution of “discontinuance” for “discontinuation”, was executed by substituting “Discontinuance” for “Discontinuation” to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 114–255
Effective Date of 2020 AmendmentAmendment by effective 180 days after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 116–136section 3112(g) of Pub. L. 116–136section 356c of this titleMar. 27, 2020
amendments: Amendments2020—Subsec. (d). added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 116–1362016—Subsec. (b)(3)(E). , which directed substitution of “discontinuance” for “discontinuation”, was executed by substituting “Discontinuance” for “Discontinuation” to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 114–255

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall maintain an up-to-date list of drugs that are determined by the Secretary to be in shortage in the United States.
The name of the drug in shortage, including the National Drug Code number for such drug.
The name of each manufacturer of such drug.

children.label: A
children.text: Requirements related to complying with good manufacturing practices.

children.label: D
children.text: Shortage of an inactive ingredient component.

children.label: E
children.text: Discontinuance of the manufacture of the drug.

children.label: F
children.text: Delay in shipping of the drug.
The estimated duration of the shortage as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (2) and (3), the Secretary shall make the information in such list publicly available.

children.heading: Trade secrets and confidential information
children.label: 2 Trade secrets and confidential information
children.text: Nothing in this section alters or amends or .section 1905 of title 18section 552(b)(4) of title 5

children.heading: Public health exception
children.label: 3 Public health exception
children.text: The Secretary may choose not to make information collected under this section publicly available under paragraph (1) or if the Secretary determines that disclosure of such information would adversely affect the public health (such as by increasing the possibility of hoarding or other disruption of the availability of drug products to patients).section 356c(c) of this title

children.heading: Interagency notification
children.label: d Interagency notification
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , and every 90 days thereafter, the Secretary shall transmit a report regarding the drugs of the current drug shortage list under this section to the Administrator of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services.March 27, 2020
